info.name: Beroya API - Route Complete Docs
info.description: Current Laravel route contract with representative examples for the old Beroya frontend. Protected routes use Sanctum bearer tokens, sales and account folders map the legacy getSales/getWeeklyAccounts/changePassword flows to the new Laravel endpoints, and multipart examples are included for file uploads.

item.name: Login

response.name: 429 Too Many Attempts

item.description: Public login endpoint. Returns a Sanctum bearer token inside `data.token` and a token expiry timestamp inside `data.token_expiry`. The old frontend still sends `currentWeekNum` and `currentYear` so the backend can seed the current week/accounts after login.

item.name: Logout
item.description: Requires only the Sanctum bearer token. The new Laravel route no longer expects CSRF headers.

request.description: Development-only bootstrap endpoint. It creates the initial admin users when `legacy.allow_admin_init` is enabled.

response.name: 200 OK - Created

item.name: Users
List Users
item.description: Returns the users visible to the authenticated admin.

response.name: 422 Validation Error
403 Forbidden

item.name: Update User Password

response.name: 422 Old Password Incorrect

item.description: Frontend note: the legacy thunk posts `id`, `old_password`, and `new_password`, but the Laravel route is now `PUT /users/{user}/password`.
User management endpoints. The frontend `changePassword` thunk now maps to `PUT /users/{user}/password`.

item.name: Sales
item.description: Legacy frontend aliases: getSales, addSale, updateSale, completeSale, approveSaleOrder, deleteSaleOrder. The Laravel routes use bearer auth, GET query parameters for list endpoints, and route params for sale IDs.

query.description: Optional for admin, recommended for gallery users.

request.description: Frontend note: `getSales` used to POST the status in the body. The Laravel route is GET with query params.

item.name: Weeks
item.description: Legacy frontend alias: getWeeks. The Laravel route is GET /weeks and requires a bearer token.

request.description: Returns the available week records ordered from newest to oldest.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

item.name: Accounts
item.description: Legacy frontend aliases: getWeeklyAccounts, updateAccountReceived, addBonus, editBonus, deleteBonus, addDeduction, editDeduction, deleteDeduction. The Laravel routes use bearer auth and route params for the primary IDs.

request.description: Frontend note: `getWeeklyAccounts` used to POST the filters. The Laravel route is GET with query params.

request.description: Returns a single account resource using the route parameter.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

request.description: Updates the account deduction amount for the selected weekly account.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

request.description: Returns the merged bonuses and deductions for the selected account. Each item includes a `type` field (`bonus` or `deduction`).
Frontend note: the Laravel route uses the account id in the URL, but it still accepts legacy `user_id` and `week_id` body fields if the frontend is still mapping by legacy identifiers.
Frontend note: `addBonus` used to send `accountant_id` in the body. The Laravel route uses the account id in the URL; the body field is still accepted as legacy compatibility.

response.name: 422 Validation Error

request.description: Frontend note: `editBonus` used to post an `id` in the body. The Laravel route uses `/bonuses/{bonus}` and only needs amount/description.

response.name: 422 Validation Error

request.description: Frontend note: `deleteBonus` used to post the id in the body. The Laravel route deletes the record directly from `/bonuses/{bonus}`.
Frontend note: `addDeduction` used to send `accountant_id` in the body. The Laravel route uses the account id in the URL; the body field is still accepted as legacy compatibility.

response.name: 422 Validation Error

request.description: Frontend note: `editDeduction` used to post an `id` in the body. The Laravel route uses `/deductions/{deduction}` and only needs amount/description.

response.name: 422 Validation Error

request.description: Frontend note: `deleteDeduction` used to post the id in the body. The Laravel route deletes the record directly from `/deductions/{deduction}`.
